evaluations.query: Design state that accumulates findings from multiple agents
expected_behavior: Uses TypedDict for defining the state schema with typed fields
Uses Annotated[list, add] for accumulating fields
Imports operator.add as the reducer function for list accumulation
Nodes return partial updates, not full state

evaluations.query: Add configuration that persists across all nodes like temperature
expected_behavior: Creates a context_schema dataclass to hold cross-node configuration
Passes the context_schema to the StateGraph constructor at initialization
Accesses shared configuration via get_context() inside individual node functions
Keeps config separate from workflow state

evaluations.query: Use Pydantic for validated state with defaults
expected_behavior: Uses Pydantic BaseModel instead of TypedDict for validated state definitions
Adds Field() with defaults and descriptions
Enables automatic validation on state updates to catch invalid data early
Handles validation errors gracefully with informative error messages to the user

evaluations.query: Add conditional routing based on whether content needs review
expected_behavior: Uses add_conditional_edges with a routing function that inspects current state
Returns a string matching one of the defined edge names from the routing function
Includes all possible routes in edge map
Has a fallback route or END condition to prevent unhandled edge cases

evaluations.query: Implement a retry loop that attempts up to 3 times then fails
expected_behavior: Tracks retry_count as an integer field in the workflow state schema
Routes back to retry node when count < 3
Routes to failure node when count >= 3
Increments the retry counter on each unsuccessful retry attempt before re-routing

evaluations.query: Create a semantic router that classifies user intent and routes accordingly
expected_behavior: Uses LLM with structured output for classification
Returns valid route names that match the defined edge map entries
Handles unknown or other category inputs with a sensible default fallback route
Uses Literal type for route validation

evaluations.query: Process a list of items in parallel with separate worker nodes
expected_behavior: Uses Send() to dispatch to workers
Returns list of Send objects from router
Each Send object includes the relevant state subset for its target worker
Workers run in parallel across all dispatched Send objects concurrently

evaluations.query: Collect results from parallel workers into a single list
expected_behavior: Uses Annotated[list, add] reducer to merge results from parallel workers
Workers return partial state updates that get merged by the reducer
Results accumulate automatically via the list reducer without manual aggregation
A single downstream node receives all accumulated results after fan-in completes

evaluations.query: Implement map-reduce: parallel processing then reduce to single output
expected_behavior: Map phase uses Send() for parallelization
Reduce node has edges from all workers
Reduce node aggregates accumulated results from all parallel worker outputs
A single final output is produced after the reduce phase completes

evaluations.query: Create a supervisor that coordinates two workers: analyzer and validator
expected_behavior: Uses StateGraph with a dedicated supervisor node that orchestrates worker routing
Creates worker nodes for analyzer and validator
Workers have edges back to supervisor
Uses add_edge(START, 'supervisor') instead of deprecated set_entry_point() for graph entry
Includes an END condition in routing logic to terminate the workflow gracefully

evaluations.query: Build a supervisor that updates state and routes in the same step
expected_behavior: Imports the Command class from langgraph.types for combined state update and routing
Supervisor returns Command with update and goto
Uses Literal type annotation for type safety
No conditional_edges needed when using Command

evaluations.query: Implement a supervisor that visits all agents exactly once before finishing
expected_behavior: Tracks the set of completed agents in the workflow state to avoid revisits
Checks available agents versus completed agents to determine the next routing target
Routes to quality_gate or END when all done
Prevents infinite loops by enforcing a maximum visit count per agent node

evaluations.query: Bind tools to an LLM and create a ToolNode for execution
expected_behavior: Uses @tool decorator for tool functions
Calls llm.bind_tools(tools) to bind the tool definitions to the LLM instance
Creates ToolNode(tools) for automatic execution of LLM-requested tool calls
Adds agent node and tool node to graph

evaluations.query: Add human approval before executing a delete tool
expected_behavior: Uses interrupt() inside the tool function to pause execution and await approval
Shows action details in interrupt payload
Checks the human approval response before executing the destructive operation
Returns a cancelled message if the human rejects the proposed tool action

evaluations.query: Select different tools based on user permissions
expected_behavior: Reads user permissions from the current state to determine allowed tool access
Filters available tools based on permissions
Binds only allowed tools to LLM
Handles case where no tools available

evaluations.query: Add PostgreSQL checkpointing to persist workflow state
expected_behavior: Uses PostgresSaver.from_conn_string() to create a persistent checkpoint backend with PostgreSQL
Passes the configured checkpointer to the graph compile() method for persistence
Sets thread_id in config for resumption
Uses async connection for async workflows

evaluations.query: Resume a workflow that was interrupted
expected_behavior: Uses same thread_id as original run
Calls invoke/ainvoke with None as input
Workflow continues execution from the last saved checkpoint state automatically
Handles case where no checkpoint exists

evaluations.query: List all checkpoints for debugging a failed workflow
expected_behavior: Uses get_state_history() method to retrieve the full sequence of checkpoint snapshots
Iterates through StateSnapshot objects to inspect state values at each checkpoint
Accesses values, next, and config from snapshots
Can identify which node failed by examining the next field in each snapshot

evaluations.query: Add human approval before executing a dangerous operation
expected_behavior: Uses the interrupt() function from langgraph.types to pause and await human input
Passes approval request data to interrupt()
Resumes workflow execution with Command(resume=response) containing the human decision
Handles both approval and rejection cases with distinct control flow branches

evaluations.query: Let human edit AI-generated content before proceeding
expected_behavior: Shows generated content in interrupt payload
Human can modify the generated content or approve it as-is before proceeding
Resume uses the human-edited version of the content for downstream processing
Original preserved if human approves as-is

evaluations.query: Implement review loop until human approves quality
expected_behavior: Tracks review_count in the workflow state to limit review iteration cycles
Calls interrupt() on each review cycle to collect human feedback before continuing
Routes back to generation if rejected
Proceeds to next step when approved

evaluations.query: Stream progress updates showing percentage complete
expected_behavior: Uses get_stream_writer() in each node function to emit custom progress events
Writes custom event with progress data
Client receives custom events via stream_mode='custom' in the stream call
Events include a structured type field and payload with progress data

evaluations.query: Stream LLM tokens to the client in real-time
expected_behavior: Uses stream_mode='messages' to receive individual LLM tokens as they are generated
LLM node uses the streaming API to emit tokens incrementally to the client
Tokens appear in the output stream as they are generated by the model
Final complete message is assembled from all received token chunks on the client

evaluations.query: Get both state updates and custom events in single stream
expected_behavior: Passes a list to stream_mode=['updates', 'custom'] to receive multiple event types simultaneously
Filters events by mode in client
Updates show state changes after each node completes its execution step
Custom mode shows user-defined progress events emitted via stream writer

evaluations.query: Call a subgraph with different state schema from parent
expected_behavior: Compiles the subgraph separately as an independent StateGraph before embedding it
Parent node transforms state before invoke
Subgraph uses its own state schema
Parent node transforms the subgraph output back into the parent state schema format

evaluations.query: Embed a subgraph that shares state with parent
expected_behavior: Subgraph uses same state schema as parent
The add_node method accepts a compiled subgraph directly as a node value
No state transformation is needed because parent and subgraph share the same schema
Subgraph reads and writes parent state fields directly without transformation logic

evaluations.query: Set up checkpointing that works across subgraphs
expected_behavior: Only the parent graph has a checkpointer configured for persisting workflow state
Uses checkpointer=False for subgraphs to delegate checkpointing to the parent graph
Subgraph state included in parent checkpoints
Resume restores subgraph state correctly from the parent graph checkpoint data

evaluations.query: Create a simple workflow using the Functional API
expected_behavior: Imports entrypoint and task from langgraph.func
Decorates individual worker functions with @task to define parallelizable units of work
Decorates the main orchestration function with @entrypoint to define the workflow entry
Calls task().result() to block and retrieve the computed return values from tasks

evaluations.query: Run multiple tasks in parallel using Functional API
expected_behavior: Calls multiple @task functions without .result()
Tasks return futures that run in parallel
Calls .result() on all futures to wait
Results are collected after parallel execution completes across all dispatched tasks

evaluations.query: Access previous state and store in a @task function
expected_behavior: Uses InjectedState type annotation to access the current checkpoint state in tasks
Accesses previous checkpoint data to read persisted state from earlier executions
Uses InjectedStore for cross-thread persistence of data across workflow invocations
Parameters annotated with Injected types are injected automatically by the LangGraph runtime

evaluations.query: I need user preferences to persist across different conversation threads. How do I store cross-thread data in LangGraph so it's not lost when starting a new thread?
expected_behavior: Uses PostgresStore for cross-thread long-term memory alongside the checkpointer
Compiles the graph with both checkpointer and store passed to compile()
Accesses store data in nodes via the store parameter with namespaced keys
Uses aget and aput methods with namespace tuples to read and write cross-thread data

evaluations.query: I have a simple three-step StateGraph pipeline with linear edges. Should I migrate it to the Functional API for cleaner code?
expected_behavior: Claude recommends migrating simple linear workflows to Functional API with @entrypoint and @task
Claude converts each node to a @task function and chains them with .result() calls
Claude warns against migrating complex topologies with conditional edges or subgraphs
Claude shows that both APIs support checkpointing, streaming, and human-in-the-loop equally

evaluations.query: I'm running three analysis agents in parallel but when one fails the entire workflow crashes and I lose the results from agents that succeeded. How do I isolate failures?
expected_behavior: Claude uses asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True to isolate branch failures
Claude adds per-branch timeout using asyncio.wait_for to prevent slow branches from blocking
Claude separates successes from failures so partial results remain available
Claude logs failures for debugging but continues processing with available successful results

evaluations.query: My chatbot agent loses previous messages every time a new node runs because the messages list gets overwritten. How do I preserve conversation history across nodes?
expected_behavior: Claude uses MessagesState or Annotated[list, add_messages] reducer for the messages field
Claude explains that add_messages appends new messages instead of overwriting the history
Claude warns that MessageGraph is deprecated in LangGraph v1.0.0 and should not be used
Claude shows nodes returning partial updates with new messages that get appended automatically

evaluations.query: Multiple nodes in my workflow update a shared config dictionary but later nodes overwrite keys set by earlier nodes. How do I merge dictionary updates instead of replacing them?
expected_behavior: Claude defines a custom merge_dicts reducer function that deep-merges dictionary updates
Claude uses Annotated[dict, merge_dicts] type annotation for the config field
Claude explains that nodes must return new state objects and never mutate state in place
Claude shows how to use Annotated[str, last_value] to make simple overwrites explicit

evaluations.query: I have a supervisor coordinating security, implementation, and tutorial agents but security should always run before implementation. How do I enforce priority-based execution order?
expected_behavior: Claude implements a priority dictionary mapping each agent to a numeric priority value
Claude selects the next agent using min() on available agents by their priority rank
Claude tracks agents_completed in state to prevent revisiting finished agents
Claude limits the supervisor to three to eight specialists maximum to avoid coordination overhead

evaluations.query: I'm manually dispatching tool calls from LLM messages in a loop. Is there a built-in way to handle tool execution with automatic parallel support in LangGraph?
expected_behavior: Claude uses the prebuilt ToolNode from langgraph.prebuilt for automatic tool execution
Claude shows that ToolNode executes multiple tool calls in parallel automatically
Claude adds a tools to agent edge for the ReAct loop pattern
Claude routes based on tool_calls presence in the last message to decide continuation

evaluations.query: I want to deploy my LangGraph agent as an API with persistence and streaming. How do I configure langgraph.json and use the CLI for local development and Docker deployment?
expected_behavior: Claude creates a langgraph.json with dependencies, graphs, and env configuration
Claude uses langgraph dev for local development with hot reload and in-memory state
Claude uses langgraph build for Docker image creation for self-hosted deployments
Claude shows the Assistants API for creating multiple configurations from a single deployed graph

evaluations.query: I have a LangGraph agent deployed on Platform and need to call it from my Python application. How do I invoke it programmatically with streaming support?
expected_behavior: Claude imports RemoteGraph from langgraph.pregel.remote for the client interface
Claude shows invoke and ainvoke methods with thread_id in config for stateful conversations
Claude demonstrates astream with stream_mode for real-time event streaming from the remote graph
Claude shows adding RemoteGraph as a node in a parent graph for distributed orchestration

evaluations.query: My child subgraph needs to escalate complex tasks back to the parent graph's supervisor node. How do I route from a subgraph node to a node in the parent graph?
expected_behavior: Claude uses Command with graph=Command.PARENT to navigate from child to parent graph
Claude includes state updates in the Command that map to the parent state schema
Claude ensures shared state keys updated via Command.PARENT have reducers in parent state
Claude warns not to wrap Command returns in try/except because it uses exceptions internally

evaluations.query: Users in my chatbot sometimes send a correction message while the previous response is still generating, causing corrupted state. How do I handle concurrent messages on the same thread?
expected_behavior: Claude sets multitask_strategy on every runs.create call to prevent race conditions
Claude explains all four strategies: reject, rollback, enqueue, and interrupt with use cases
Claude recommends rollback for chatbots where user corrections should override in-progress work
Claude warns that without a multitask_strategy concurrent messages on the same thread corrupt state

evaluations.query: My LangGraph workflow crashes on the first node because the state has no messages yet and I'm accessing state['messages'][-1]. How do I handle empty initial state?
expected_behavior: Claude adds a guard check for empty lists before accessing the last element of state fields
Claude uses default values in TypedDict or Pydantic model for fields that may be empty at startup
Claude does not assume state fields are populated when the first node runs after graph initialization
Claude shows a pattern for initializing required state fields in the entry node before downstream access

evaluations.query: One of my LangGraph nodes calls an external API that sometimes throws ConnectionError. The entire workflow crashes and I lose all progress. How do I add fault tolerance?
expected_behavior: Claude wraps the external API call in try/except inside the node function to catch transient errors
Claude writes an error status to state so downstream nodes can detect and handle the failure gracefully
Claude uses checkpointing so that progress is saved before the failing node and can be resumed
Claude does not let unhandled exceptions propagate and destroy the entire workflow execution state

evaluations.query: My LangGraph agent takes free-form user input and passes it directly to a tool node. A user submitted input that tricked the LLM into calling a delete tool. How do I prevent this?
expected_behavior: Claude implements a tool allowlist that restricts which tools the agent can invoke per conversation context
Claude adds human-in-the-loop approval via interrupt() before executing any destructive tool operations
Claude validates tool call arguments against expected schemas before forwarding to the ToolNode
Claude does not pass raw unvalidated user input directly into tool descriptions or function arguments
